name: Histogram POP
description: The Histogram POP takes an attribute from its input, creates an output with one attribute (Count attribute) and a specified number of points (Number of Bins parameter), and for each input point, adds 1 to one of the bins. This gives a "frequency distribution". It divides the entire range of values (Input Range parameter) into a series of intervals, and then counts how many values fall into each interval (bin). Values of the input that are outside the lower and upper range of the bins can be ignored, clamped to the min or max, or added to two extra bins that count values out of range. It can also output the interval start values into a separate attribute BinValue. By it nature, it works on a single component of a vector attribute, such as P(1). You may want to first convert a vector to a float using length() in an Math POP. You can histogram point attributes or primitive attributes. The result is often viewed with a [[POP to CHOP]], or with the popViewer component which can show an attribute as a graph. The Histogram POP takes all the values of a single component attribute, for example P(1) and for each input point, adds 1 to a bin of the output. The output has N bins which is actually N points with one attribute Count. The user specifies the value that the first bin represents, and the value that the last bin represents, and using N, the Histogram POP determines the low and high values represented by each bin. Count starts at 0 for all bins, and the appropriate bin gets incremented by 1 for each input points. See also: [[Accumulate POP]], [[Analyze POP]], [[ReRange POP]]

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Scalar input attribute or vector attribute component.

parameters.name: Use Input Range
parameters.label: Use Input Range
parameters.description: Specify teh range of the input attribute values. Default range is 0 to the number of bins.

parameters.name: Input Range
parameters.label: Input Range
parameters.description: When used, the input range gets divided in to Number of Bins.

parameters.name: Output Bin Value
parameters.label: Output Bin Value
parameters.description: Whether to output a separate attribute with the bin values, when using an input range.

parameters.name: Quantize
parameters.label: Quantize
parameters.description: Convert values into a finite set of discrete levels.

parameters.name: Outside Range
parameters.label: Outside Range
parameters.description: Determines the behavior outside the input range.

parameters.name: Number of Bins
parameters.label: Number of Bins
parameters.description: The number of bins to sort the attribute values in.

parameters.name: Count Attribute
parameters.label: Count Attribute
parameters.description: Sets the attribute scope when outputting the count attribute
